title: Visual & Sonic Curator,
description: A storyteller and explorer of Art, Music, Culture and the Wild and it's life.  I specialize in curating expressive spaces, innovative projects, and interdisciplinary art events that connect culture, music, people, and environment. Art is an experience, and everyday is different.

title: Mzizi ni Msingi
description: Mzizi ni Msingi, is a project to explore Tanzania history in the cultural and creative scene through photography, music and design as influenced by different eras and historical events. Tanzania is a diverse country when it comes to culture and traditions, with over 120 tribes. Its history has definitely been affected by time, experience, events and social dynamics from pre and post independence era. This project is a sonic and visual journey through memory lane for old generations and history and knowledge for the new generations. As we explore through different mediums some will be entertained and reminisce others will learn and relate and those before will be celebrated. The project provides space and time for interaction, reflections and socialisations between generations.

title: Disability Empowerment Through Art(DETA)
description: If a perfect Utopia existed everybody would have a ground to dance, sing and act their own stories. Earth isn’t that, it’s selective and sometimes we miss great narrations. For Nipael though this has been different she has been playing her story on the piano and crafting it into soulful jewelleries since she discovered ART.  As an artist she has always been mindful that her ART transmits positive energy and is therapeutic. Under Nipa foundation she has been using her craft to reach out to those that have been less fortunate to express themselves because of different disabilities. As an ARTIST who herself has a platform to express her thoughts and ideas she created Disability Empowerment Through Art (DETA).

title: The Nest_Climate Justice Camp Arusha, 2024
description: The Nest at the Climate Justice Camp  was crafted as a free space, a welcoming refuge from the structured agenda—here, we could unwind, create, and communicate in ways that transcended the limitations of language.

title: Kizamani Kisasa & At the Brigde_yours is mine
description: These are exihinbition both static and interactive that happened at Nafasi Art Space, the exihibiton Kizamani Kisasa was to explore the past and the present era through artistic works and collection of antics. At the Bridge, yours is mine was a interactive workshop which used Art to explore connection and communication between people from different cultures. The activities were part of Nafasi Art Space art intiatives and plan to develop Art in Tanzania

title: VIFICHWAVYO” or “THE HIDDEN
description: Hidden narratives in everyday objects. This collection shifts the focus away from grand monuments to the overlooked minutiae of daily life. By stripping away context through extreme close-ups and dramatic lighting, ordinary items become mysterious and unrecognizable. A discarded piece of metal or a peeling wall is elevated to the status of a cryptic artifact. The intention is to challenge the viewer's automatic categorization of their surroundings and invite a deeper, more imaginative level of engagement. What is obscure is not empty; it is simply waiting to be seen from a new, untethered perspective. The work suggests that profound stories can be found in the most unassuming of places if we only look closely enough.

title: Chaza wa ghuba kwa chumvi ya uvinza
description:
Culture is dynamic and live archive shaped and changing with time, people's experience, interactions, reactions, and how they choose to express themselves. “Chaza wa ghuba kwa chumvi ya uvinza” is an immersive experience that traces the trails and exposes the visuals and sounds that have told the Tanzania lifestyle tales.
 It’s the rhythms from every step taken by our fathers from Kigoma to Pwani, revolts of expressions from Northern to Southern Highlands, artistic expression from the basin of Eyasi. The shapes and structures around us have changed with time, which has affected our spaces and perspectives.

title: Music Connect Africa
description: This is a regional programme led by British council aimed at strengthening creative and cultural industry based in the music sector in Sub Saharan Africa. The goal is to suppport economic and social development focusing on the youth and under represented communities.

title: Connect for Culture Africa
description: CfCA is a regional initiative at transforming Africa’s Arts, culture and heritage through advocacy and lobbying for increased public investment, with a goal of allocating at least 1% of national budgets by 2030. The initiative integrates advocacy efforts at both regional and national levels, fostering a multistakeholder network of cultural advocates
